failure_modes.text: Ownership can keep teams defending what they already have without seriously comparing it to the best available alternative today.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Ownership Logic Hides Opportunity Cost
failure_modes.mitigation: Force a zero-base comparison: ask whether the team would acquire this asset today at its current cost if they did not already own it.

failure_modes.text: Leaders can fail to quit, divest, or switch because ownership inflates the perceived value of the status quo.
failure_modes.mitigation: Build in explicit quit, divest, or switch checkpoints; ownership inflates perceived value and makes exit feel like loss even when it is gain.

failure_modes.text: Motivated reasoning can justify an owned asset's value with narrative confidence rather than neutral facts.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require neutral facts — not narrative confidence — to justify holding an owned asset; motivated reasoning is the endowment effect's enforcement mechanism.

premortem_questions.text: If we did not already own this asset, idea, or project, how much would we pay to acquire it today?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If I did not currently own this asset, how much would I be willing to pay

premortem_questions.text: What facts, independent of our history or feeling of ownership, support this valuation?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What facts, independent of my history or feeling of ownership, support this valuation?

premortem_questions.text: What assumptions about value would prove wrong if this transaction or hold decision failed?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this transaction were to fail, what assumptions about value did I hold that proved incorrect?

heuristics.text: Ask whether it is true value or just ownership talking before defending a held asset or plan.
heuristics.source_quote: Do I really value this, or is it just the ownership talking?

heuristics.text: Anchor first to the outside view so the market or neutral benchmark disciplines the inside view.
Write the valuation or choice down in explicit terms so emotion has to compete with a fact-based plan.
heuristics.source_quote: Draft a Plan and Write Out Thoughts

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on ownership distortion, divestment failure, outside-view valuation, and anti-opportunity-cost blindness.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader behavioral-economics sprawl because the raw source is stronger on concrete valuation correction and attachment discipline.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses several source passages on written plans and explicit valuation so the chunk stays operational.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on anchoring, rationalization, and opportunity-cost because ownership changes the reference point and invites self-justifying stories.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on sunk-cost-fallacy, status-quo-bias, and first-principles-thinking because those models expose when attachment outruns facts.
structured_tensions: The source has explicit structured tensions with sunk-cost-fallacy, status-quo-bias, and first-principles-thinking that cleanly map to the current target-id surface.
